purpose: Per-task outcome record for the TASK-HMIG-010 feature-level LangGraph validation run. Populated by the operator after each task completes (the runner does not yet auto-emit this — see TASK-FIX-CANARY-PARSER for the analogous gap on the canary side).

run_attempts.notes: Aborted at Wave 1 / TASK-FIX-IA03 / Turn 1 with LangGraphHarnessError (auth). Root cause: feature subcommand doesn't thread --model. See incident I-001 (F9) in feature-run-incidents.md and TASK-FIX-LGFM.

run_attempts.halted_by: stop_on_failure=True after Wave 1 task 1 errored on turn 2
run_attempts.notes: Post-LGFM run. Surfaced F10 (main Player still gets model=None — sibling-of-F9 in AgentInvoker._invoke_task_work_implement, line 5730) and F11 (DeepAgents conversation_history offload to read-only /conversation_history/ host root, sibling-of-NOVMODE). Both filed as TASK-FIX-LGFM2 and TASK-FIX-CHO01. Coach successfully ran for ~1.5min on synthetic report (with model='openai:qwen36-workhorse' — proving Coach side of model threading is correct), but main Player path consumed 2 turns failing identically. See I-002 and I-003 in incidents.

run_attempts.halted_by: stop_on_failure=True after Wave 2 had 1 task fail (GD02 timeout-then-approved divergence)
run_attempts.feature_status_caveat: STRICT letter reading is FAILED. GD02's true verdict is AMBIGUOUS due to F14 cancellation race (outer says timeout/failed, inner Coach says approved). AC-008 verdict computation deferred until F14 (CTOUT01) lands and run 4 produces a clean signal.
run_attempts.notes: First run producing real autobuild data. Findings: F12 (sibling-of-F10, coach_test role missing model, soft-fails to subprocess), F13 (substrate-quality: test-orchestrator hits SPECHANG 600s cap on qwen36-workhorse), F14 (cancellation race makes GD02 verdict ambiguous — blocks AC-008 computation), F15 (substrate-quality: GD02 needed 50min for complexity-6 multi-turn iteration), F16 (cosmetic: Graphiti FalkorDB teardown race). F11 (CHO01) fix empirically validated — MODEL-PROFILE summarization fires at ~111k, no context overflow this run. See incidents I-004 through I-006.

run_attempts.halted_by: stop_on_failure=True after Wave 1 IA03 hit 'Coach decision not found' hard-fail
run_attempts.feature_status_caveat: Hard-fail at Wave 1 verdict-emission. Substantive Player work was CORRECT (14/14 doc-level exclusion tests passing per phase_4_summary.json) but Coach LLM didn't emit the Bash-heredoc verdict file. F17 root-caused: canary F2 at Coach level + orchestrator load-bearing inconsistency. AC-008 verdict computation still blocked; awaits TASK-FIX-COACHSF01.
run_attempts.clean_executions_per_orchestrator: 1/1 (100%) — Player + specialists succeeded; Coach failed at verdict emission
run_attempts.first_pass_success_rate_inclusive_of_sub: n/a (substantive Player work was correct; verdict-emission was the failure mode)
run_attempts.notes: Run 4 was the first run with CTOUT01 (F14) + LGFM3 (F12) landed. CTOUT01 was not exercised (no Coach approval to race against cancellation). LGFM3 confirmed working (no model=None on coach_test SDK test execution; no fall-back-to-subprocess log line). F13 (test-orchestrator SPECHANG) did NOT fire — specialist completed in ~300s. New findings: F17 (Coach verdict-emission failure, blocks AC-008, TASK-FIX-COACHSF01 filed), F18 (pip-cache ghost-path filter gap, cosmetic, recorded as I-008). See incidents I-007, I-008.

run_attempts.halted_by: stop_on_failure=True after Wave 1 IA03 task-level 3000s timeout fired (CTOUT01 cancellation propagated cleanly)
run_attempts.feature_status_caveat: Substrate-sizing failure under gemma4-coach (F20: n_ctx=65536 too small for code-reviewer specialist payloads; observed at 69174 tokens). F21 turn-2 Coach hard-stall is downstream consequence of F20. Architecture invariants all confirmed working: COACHSF01 turn-1 synthetic feedback fired correctly; CTOUT01 turn-2 cancellation propagated correctly; selector colon-alias (d526bf0f) and --coach-model plumbing (d07a4209) both end-to-end. AC-006 of TASK-HMIG-013 not met (Coach verdict-emission rate this run = 0% natural). Operator-side llama-swap n_ctx bump (98304 or 131072) unblocks; no code edits required.
run_attempts.clean_executions_per_orchestrator: 1/1 (100%) — Player + turn-1 + turn-2 Player + turn-1 Coach (synthetic) completed cleanly; turn-2 Coach hung, cancelled within task-timeout window
run_attempts.first_pass_success_rate_inclusive_of_sub: n/a (substrate-sizing failure prevented Coach verdict signal on turn 2; turn-1 substantive Player work was correct, recovered by COACHSF01 synthetic feedback)
run_attempts.notes: First run under TASK-HMIG-013 substrate posture (Player=qwen36-workhorse, Coach=gemma4:26b). Run 7 hit the selector colon-alias bug (closed by d526bf0f); run 8 is the first to actually reach gemma4-coach Coach payload depth. Architecture invariants from runs 1-7 ALL confirmed working: COACHSF01 (F17 fix) fired exactly as designed on turn 1 — Coach emitted 4898 chars content + 0 reasoning, no fenced JSON → synthetic feedback emitted → Player produced substantive 46-file turn-2 retry. CTOUT01 (F14 fix) fired exactly as designed on turn 2 — Coach hung 990s+, harness.cancel() propagated, task CANCELLED at 3000s boundary. New findings: F20 (gemma4-coach n_ctx=65536 too small, HIGH operator-fixable, I-009 filed), F21 (turn-2 Coach hard-stall downstream of F20, MEDIUM substrate-quality, I-010 filed). AC-009 NOT exercised (--reasoning off; the 4898-char prose was the COACHSF01-handled shape, not the AC-009 surface). Operator-side mitigation: llama-swap n_ctx bump 65536 → 98304 (recommended) or 131072. Re-run 9 with --fresh after llama-swap reload.

run_attempts.halted_by: stop_on_failure=True after Wave 1 IA03 task-level 3000s timeout fired (CTOUT01 cancellation propagated cleanly; this time the timeout was legitimate time-budget exhaustion, not a substrate hang)
run_attempts.feature_status_caveat: F20 + F21 from run 8 are both RESOLVED empirically (operator-side llama-swap n_ctx bump landed). Architecture invariants from runs 1-8 still working. Run 9 failed because (a) F17 substrate F2-at-Coach-level persists under --reasoning off (turn-1 Coach produced 25211 chars prose, no fenced JSON; COACHSF01 caught it correctly), and (b) F13 SPECHANG (turn-2 test-orchestrator hit 600s cap with the larger Player diff) ate the turn-2 time budget before Coach could complete. AC-006 still not met. AC-009 (COACHBUDG01 parser robustness with --reasoning auto) is the next experiment.
run_attempts.clean_executions_per_orchestrator: 1/1 (100%) — Player turn 1 (298s) + turn-1 specialists (~730s) + Coach turn 1 (944s, synthetic feedback) + Player turn 2 (320s) all completed cleanly; turn-2 test-orchestrator hit 600s SPECHANG; turn-2 Coach got ~80s before legitimate task-timeout cancellation
run_attempts.first_pass_success_rate_inclusive_of_sub: n/a (both Player turns substantively succeeded; failure was the F17 verdict-emission pattern persisting under --reasoning off, compounded by F13 SPECHANG time-budget pressure)

f17_evidence.delta_vs_run_8: 5.1x more prose output in 1.7x the wall-time; the larger n_ctx let deepagents feed Coach a richer prompt and gemma4 responded with proportionally longer prose, but still didn't converge on the fenced-JSON contract under --reasoning off

f13_compounding_evidence.observation: test-orchestrator wall time scales with Player diff size; with 45-file diff on turn 2 it hits the 600s SPECHANG cap, consuming the turn-2 budget before Coach can complete

run_attempts.notes: F20 + F21 RESOLVED: zero HTTP 400 / exceed_context_size_error in run-9 (grep-confirmed). Architecture invariants all still working: COACHSF01 (turn 1 synthetic feedback fired correctly), CTOUT01 (turn 2 cancellation propagated cleanly at legitimate 3000s task-timeout), d526bf0f selector, d07a4209 --coach-model plumbing. F17 now the dominant load-bearing constraint. Next experiment: run 10 with --reasoning auto (tests AC-009 / COACHBUDG01 parser-fallback empirically) + --task-timeout 4500 (absorbs F13 SPECHANG variance). If run 10 produces >=95% Coach verdict-emission rate, AC-006/AC-009 victory and TASK-HMIG-011 cutover unblocks. If not, escalation path is nemotron-3-super:120b-a12b (gated on 2nd GB10 + ConnectX-7).

task_outcomes.notes: Failed at Player turn 1 with LangGraphHarnessError(model=None, auth). Not a substrate-quality finding — this is a CLI-plumbing gap (F9 / TASK-FIX-LGFM). After LGFM lands, re-run with --fresh.

Post-LGFM run. Main Player still fails with model=None (F10, AgentInvoker._invoke_task_work_implement missing model= threading). Synthetic report fired; test-orchestrator specialist DID get model='openai:qwen36-workhorse' (proving the Coach + specialist paths got LGFM correctly), but immediately hit F11 (DeepAgents conversation_history offload fails to read-only host root /conversation_history/, summarization can't trim, prompt grows to 569k tokens, llama-swap returns 400 exceed_context_size). Two new blockers filed: TASK-FIX-LGFM2 (F10) and TASK-FIX-CHO01 (F11).

First real autobuild approval. Player ran 246.8s with 48 messages (1 assistant, 0 tools tracked by LangGraph counter — note: tool use under DeepAgents may not increment the SDK-style counter; git detection found 4 modified + 43 created files, of which 4 ghost paths were filtered, leaving ~36 real Player-created files). Test-orchestrator specialist HIT 600s SPECHANG cap (F13 substrate-quality finding) but Coach approved on synthetic report + Graphiti context. Coach turn 1 logged F12 coach_test model=None error but subprocess fallback succeeded. Quality gates ALL_PASSED=True. APPROVED in 1 turn.
Cleanest pass of the run. Approved on turn 1 within Wave 2. No findings recorded. This is the canonical 'substrate is working' data point.

task_outcomes.first_pass_success_caveat: Turn 1 Coach gave FEEDBACK (honesty discrepancy: Player reported 6 files modified but git status showed none of those). Turn 2 Coach approved after Player corrected. So first-pass-success was FALSE under any reading; what's ambiguous is whether turn 2 should count as 'recovered successfully' or 'timed out'.
task_outcomes.non_recoverable_caveat: Outer orchestrator marks as task_timeout (non-recoverable per AC-006). Inner Coach reached APPROVED state ~48s after the cancellation fired. F14 (cancellation race) makes the true verdict ambiguous. Blocks AC-008 computation.
task_outcomes.notes: F14 cancellation race. Substrate-quality finding F15 (50min for complexity-6 multi-turn iteration on qwen36-workhorse — substrate is slow on this task shape). After CTOUT01 lands, re-run will produce an unambiguous verdict. Possible mitigation for run 4: bump --task-timeout 3000 -> 4500 to give GD02 more runway, OR bump SPECHANG cap if test-orchestrator timing is a contributing factor.

task_outcomes.first_pass_success_caveat: Player substantively succeeded: 14/14 new doc-level exclusion tests passing per phase_4_summary.json; 517/580 suite tests passed (63 pre-existing failures unrelated to fix, honestly disclosed in output_summary). The failure was Coach LLM not emitting the Bash-heredoc verdict file — substrate substrate-quality F2-at-Coach-level finding. Player work itself was good.
task_outcomes.non_recoverable_caveat: Hard-fail because the orchestrator's synthetic-feedback safety net at autobuild.py:5663 doesn't fire when invoke_coach returns success=False (only on raised exceptions). After TASK-FIX-COACHSF01 wires the safety net to fire on 'Coach decision not found' results, this failure mode will become a soft-fail feedback turn instead.
task_outcomes.notes: F17 hard-blocker. Player + specialists ran cleanly. LGFM3 (F12 fix) confirmed working — no model=None on coach_test SDK test execution. CTOUT01 (F14 fix) not exercised (no Coach approval to race against cancellation). F18 cosmetic: 40+ pip-cache ghost paths in files_modified list (recorded as I-008).

task_outcomes.first_pass_success_caveat: Turn 1 Player substantively succeeded (41 files created, 1 modified in 213s). Turn 1 Coach hit F2-at-Coach-level (4898 chars content, 0 reasoning, no fenced JSON) — COACHSF01 synthetic feedback fired correctly. Turn 2 Player produced substantive 46-file recovery in 197s. Turn 2 code-reviewer specialist hit HTTP 400 exceed_context_size_error (F20: 69174 tokens > gemma4-coach n_ctx=65536). Turn 2 Coach validation then hung 990s+ with zero successful HTTP calls (F21, downstream of F20). CTOUT01 cancellation fired cleanly at 3000s task-timeout. So substantive Player work was correct both turns; failure was substrate-sizing on Coach payload.
task_outcomes.non_recoverable_caveat: F20 root cause is operator-side llama-swap config (n_ctx=65536), NOT a code defect. Per AC-006 definition ('any failure the operator cannot resolve without code edits to the harness itself'), F20 is RECOVERABLE — operator bumps n_ctx in llama-swap and re-runs. Substrate-sizing fixes do not require code edits.
task_outcomes.notes: First TASK-HMIG-013-posture run (Player=qwen36-workhorse, Coach=gemma4:26b). Architecture invariants from runs 1-7 ALL confirmed: COACHSF01 (F17 fix) + CTOUT01 (F14 fix) + d526bf0f selector colon-alias + d07a4209 --coach-model plumbing + LGFM3/MODEL-PROFILE/ghost-path filter all working as designed. F20 + F21 are substrate findings (recorded as I-009 and I-010); operator-side llama-swap n_ctx bump (98304 recommended first step, or 131072) unblocks. AC-009 (--reasoning auto parser robustness) NOT exercised this run.

task_outcomes.first_pass_success_caveat: Turn 1 Player substantively succeeded (1 file created, 2 modified in 298s). Turn 1 Coach produced 25211 chars prose, 0 reasoning, no fenced JSON — F17 substrate F2-at-Coach persistence under --reasoning off; COACHSF01 synthetic feedback fired correctly. Turn 2 Player produced substantive 45-file recovery in 320s. Turn 2 test-orchestrator specialist hit 600s F13 SPECHANG cap (larger Player diff). Turn 2 Coach got ~80s before task-level 3000s timeout fired — legitimate time-budget exhaustion, NOT F21-style substrate hang. CTOUT01 cancellation propagated cleanly. F20 + F21 from run 8 both RESOLVED empirically (zero HTTP 400 / exceed_context_size_error this run).
task_outcomes.non_recoverable_caveat: F17 persistence + F13 SPECHANG compounding are substrate-quality findings, not code defects. Per AC-006 definition ('any failure the operator cannot resolve without code edits to the harness itself'), RECOVERABLE — operator flips gemma4-coach to --reasoning auto (exercises COACHBUDG01 AC-009 parser-fallback) and bumps --task-timeout to 4500s.
task_outcomes.notes: Architecture invariants from runs 1-8 ALL still working: COACHSF01 + CTOUT01 + selector colon-alias + --coach-model plumbing + LGFM3 + MODEL-PROFILE + ghost-path filter. F20 + F21 RESOLVED by §9.13 n_ctx bump. F17 (25211 chars prose, 5.1x run-8) is now dominant. Next run: --reasoning auto + --task-timeout 4500.

task_outcome_schema.first_pass_success: true|false (bool, required) — true iff coach_decision == 'approve' AND turns_used == 1
task_outcome_schema.resume_used: true|false (bool, required) — true iff a --resume invocation was needed
task_outcome_schema.non_recoverable: true|false (bool, required) — true iff failure required code edits to the harness itself per AC-006 definition
task_outcome_schema.notes: free-form string, optional — failure analysis, resume rationale, divergence-from-canary commentary
